meaning: n.谣言,传闻,道听途说adj.传闻的
examples: Much of what was reported to them was hearsay.
We can't make a decision based on hearsay and guesswork.
He urged the committee to discount any secondhand knowledge or hearsay.
Prosecutors believe her testimony about Kevorkian's intent to ease her husband's pain would be hearsay.
Rogue employees could do even more damage, says Clara Shih, the founder of Hearsay Social Inc.
